stem: If an algorithm is O(n) and takes 1 second for 1,000 items, roughly how long for 10,000 items?
explanation_after: O(n) = linear time. If input grows 10x, time grows 10x. 10,000 items = ~10 seconds. This is why you must predict if code will work at scale.

stem: Attention in a transformer is O(n²) where n = sequence length. If processing 512 tokens takes 1 unit of compute, how much does 2048 tokens take?
explanation_after: 2048/512 = 4x more tokens. O(n²) means compute grows by 4² = 16x. This quadratic scaling is why long-context models are so expensive.

prompt: An algorithm that always takes the same amount of time regardless of input size is O(______)
explanation_after: O(1) = constant time. Dictionary lookup is O(1). Accessing a list element by index is O(1). These operations don't get slower as data grows.
